title: Alsine picta
intro: Alsine picta, also known as paintwort or sorrel-scented stitchwort, is a flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Alsine picta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Alsine picta is commonly used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into syrups and cough drops to help soothe respiratory tract irritations.
taxonomy: Alsine picta is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink saxifrage. The genus Alsine belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae, which comprises over 460 species of annual and perennial plants. Alsine picta is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is often found growing in rocky or sandy areas. This plant is known for its pink or purple flowers and is typically low-growing, forming small clusters or mats. Alsine picta is also known as the pink saxifrage or Alsine pink saxifrage and is a member of the order Caryophyllales.
